title: List of National Treasures of Japan (crafts: others)
rows: Handed down in the Kanzen-in ( 観禅院 ) subtemple ; contains an inscription
Bell ; bronze ; aperture : 89.2 cm ( 35.1 in ) , height : 149.0 cm ( 58.7 in )
Nara Nara Kofukuji Kōfuku-ji , Nara , Nara

Cast for Kinseki-ji ( 金石寺 ) in Hōki Province （in Tottori Prefecture ） ; moved in 1653 to Izumo-taisha , then in 1889 to Tafuku-ji ( 多福寺 ) , Matsubashi-ji ( 松林寺 ) in Shimane Prefecture until being dedicated in 1897 to Saikō-ji
Bell ; aperture : 77.5 cm ( 30.5 in ) , height : 136.4 cm ( 53.7 in )
Fukuoka Fukuoka Saikoji Saikō-ji ( 西光寺 ) , Fukuoka , Fukuoka

Also called Tsurezuregusa ( 徒然草 ) or Ōjikichō ( 黄鐘調 ) ( scale in gagaku , similar to Dorian mode on A ) bell ; fundamental frequency : 129 Hz ; together with the bell at Kanzeon-ji , one of the oldest extant Japanese bells
Bell ; bronze ; aperture : 87 cm ( 34 in ) , height : 124 cm ( 49 in )
Kyoto Kyoto Myoshinji Lecture Hall ( 法堂 , hōdō ) at Myōshin-ji , Kyoto
Without inscription ; cast in Tatara ( 多々良 ) , Kasuya District , Fukuoka , Chikuzen Province using the same mold as the National Treasure bell at Myōshin-ji ; one of the oldest extant Japanese bells
Bell ; bronze ; aperture : 86 cm ( 34 in ) , height : 106 cm ( 42 in )
Fukuoka Dazaifu Kanzeonji Kanzeon-ji , Dazaifu , Fukuoka
Without inscription； long and narrow shape； ｗith cracks and Japanese Honeysuckle arabesque pattern
Bell ; bronze ; aperture : 85 cm ( 33 in ) , height : 150 cm ( 59 in )
Nara Katsuragi Taimadera belfry ( shōrō ) at Taima-dera , Katsuragi , Nara
Without inscription ; biggest bell in Japan
Bell ; aperture : 271 cm ( 107 in ) , height : 385 cm ( 152 in ) , weight : 49 t ( 48 long tons ; 54 short tons )
Nara Nara Todaiji belfry ( shōrō ) at Tōdai-ji , Nara , Nara
Dedicated by Dōkyō together with a sacred horse ( 神馬 , shinme ) ; contains inscription
Bell ; aperture : 73.9 cm ( 29.1 in ) , height : 109.9 cm ( 43.3 in ) , width : 88.5 cm ( 34.8 in ) , thickness at aperture : 5.8 cm ( 2.3 in )
Fukui Echizen Tsurugi Shrine Tsurugi Shrine ( 劔神社 ) , Echizen , Fukui
With inscription by Fujiwara no Toshiyuki
Bell ; bronze ; aperture : 80.5 cm ( 31.7 in ) , height : 148 cm ( 58 in )
Kyoto Kyoto Jingoji belfry ( shōrō ) at Jingo-ji , Kyoto
With calligraphy attributed to Ono no Michikaze
Bell ; bronze ; diameter : 90 cm ( 35 in ) , height : 150 cm ( 59 in )
Nara Gojo Eisanji Eisan-ji ( 栄山寺 ) , Gojō , Nara
Decorated with lion , dragon , Chinese phoenix and dancing heavenly nymphs ; previously located in the belfry ( shōrō ) ; one of the Three Great Bells of Japan
Bell ; aperture : 123 cm ( 48 in ) , height : 199 cm ( 78 in ) , weight : 2 t ( 2.0 long tons ; 2.2 short tons )
Kyoto Uji Byodoin Byōdō-in Museum Hōshōkan ( 平等院ミュージアム鳳翔館 ) , Byōdō-in , Uji , Kyoto
Inscription in embossed carving by founder Lanxi Daolong ; donated by Hōjō Tokiyori
Kanagawa Kamakura Kenchoji belfry ( shōrō ) at Kenchō-ji , Kamakura , Kanagawa
Made by order of Hōjō Sadatoki ; largest bell in Kantō ; donated by Hōjō Tokiyori
Kanagawa Kamakura Engakuji Engaku-ji , Kamakura , Kanagawa
Initially at the west pagoda of Enryaku-ji 's Hōdō-in ( 宝幢院 ) ; contains a three line , 24 characters inscription
Bell ; aperture : 55.3 cm ( 21.8 in ) , height : 116.0 cm ( 45.7 in )
Shiga Moriyama Sagawa Art Museum Sagawa Art Museum , Moriyama , Shiga
Head in dragon design , body decorated with clouds and celestial beings ; oldest Korean bell in Japan
Bell ; aperture : 66.7 cm ( 26.3 in ) , height : 112 cm ( 44 in )
Fukui Tsuruga Jogu Shrine Treasure House at Jōgū Shrine ( 常宮神社 , Jōgū jinja ) , Tsuruga , Fukui

description: This table lists National Treasures of Japan categorized under crafts, specifically focusing on items such as bells and other artifacts. It provides details about the name, artists, historical context, physical characteristics, and current location of each treasure. The table serves as a reference for cultural and historical preservation efforts.
